conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: When we launched our AI startup, I believed that bootstrapping the company from customer revenue was the only way to maintain true operational freedom.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Bootstrapping keeps you in complete control of your equity and product roadmap.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But compute costs for LLMs are insane. I quickly realized we couldn't compete, so I shifted my belief and argued that raising a massive Venture Capital seed round was mandatory to survive the foundational model race.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: VC capital is crucial when you have intensive capital requirements early on.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The problem with venture capital is the hyper-dilution and loss of control to board members who don't understand the tech. I've completely reframed my perspective. I now strongly believe that venture debt and strategic enterprise partnerships, rather than standard equity-based venture capital, is the correct financing model for modern AI software businesses.

query: What is the user's current belief regarding the best way to fund an AI startup?
